title: FIIG Securities Limited — Regulatory action
investigation_objective: The Federal Court ordered FIIG Securities Limited to pay a $2.5 million penalty plus $500,000 towards ASIC's costs — the first civil penalty imposed on an Australian Financial Services licensee for cybersecurity-related contraventions of its general licensee obligations under section 912A of the Corporations Act 2001. FIIG admitted that over a four-year period it failed to have adequate cybersecurity measures in place, which contributed to a May 2023 cyberattack in which approximately 385GB of data, including sensitive client information, was exfiltrated from its systems.
scope: Federal Court civil penalty of $2.5 million, admitted by the company — the first such penalty for cybersecurity-related licensee obligation failures in Australia; Admitted inadequate cybersecurity measures over a four-year period prior to a May 2023 data breach affecting approximately 385GB of data including sensitive client information; Court also ordered an independent-expert compliance programme to review its cyber security and resilience systems
